description: Two distinct natural-voice speakers (piper en_US-ryan + en_US-amy) alternating in a podcast-style intro. 8 turns, ~30 s of audio. If diarization works at all on natural synthesized audio, the two voices should cluster cleanly.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: Welcome back to the show. Today we have a special guest with us.

turns.speaker: guest
turns.text: Thanks so much for having me. It's a real pleasure to be here today.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: Let's start with the basics. Can you tell our listeners what you do?

turns.speaker: guest
turns.text: Of course. I work on real time speech recognition systems and large neural networks.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: That sounds fascinating. What got you interested in that field originally?

turns.speaker: guest
turns.text: I've always been curious about how computers can understand what people are saying.

turns.speaker: host
turns.text: And what's the hardest part of that problem in your view?

turns.speaker: guest
turns.text: Without a doubt, knowing who is speaking when multiple people are talking together.
